hypothesis: Replacing the template-based natural language generation pipeline with adversarial prompt augmentation—where a separate language model is trained to generate maximally diverse and semantically ambiguous prompts for the same physical structure—will disproportionately reduce the generalization gap between training-paraphrase and held-out validation performance (currently ΔE ≈1 vs. ≈34) compared to further scaling decoder depth, because the dominant source of validation error is linguistic distributional shift rather than insufficient model capacity for sequence generation.
